symptom: Photo shows a downstream panel where neutral and equipment grounding conductors may be mixed, bonded, or isolated incorrectly.
diagnosis: Subpanel bonding issue. In a downstream panel, neutrals must be isolated from the enclosure and equipment grounds must bond to the enclosure.
procedure: Identify whether this is service equipment or a downstream panel.
Kill feeder power and verify dead before touching bars or conductors.
Check whether the neutral bar is isolated from the metal enclosure.
Check whether the equipment grounding bar is bonded to the enclosure.
Separate neutral and equipment grounding conductors if this is a downstream panel.
safety_notes: Do not bond neutral and ground in a downstream panel.
Do not remove the service bond at service equipment.
If the feeder lacks an equipment grounding conductor, stop and evaluate the feeder.
